title: Good: ipv6_addr.h struct in6_addr detection cascade
description:
Location: ipv6_addr.h:42-62

The header uses a multi-stage detection strategy for struct in6_addr:
1. LICHEN_HAVE_IN6_ADDR user override
2. IN6ADDR_ANY_INIT (POSIX-defined macro)
3. Zephyr-specific: CONFIG_NET_IPV6
4. Various platform header guards
5. Fallback definition

Why this is good: Cross-platform IPv6 code notoriously breaks on 'struct in6_addr already defined' or 'struct in6_addr not defined'. This cascade handles the common cases (POSIX, Zephyr, standalone) and provides escape hatches (LICHEN_HAVE_IN6_ADDR) for edge cases. The comment explaining each detection method aids debugging when porting to a new platform.
